states: Habit and domain forms in code
source inspection only; demo user lacked admin access

topFindings.finding: White-theme Week View loses readable core content because the page bypasses semantic theme tokens.
Enabled Calories and Achievements routes redirect to Today on a cold deep link before settings resolve.
Today presents useful data but not one coherent decision model for focus, obligations, capacity, progress, and next action.
Module behavior is spread across route, navigation, Add, Today, Week, Settings, and analytics conditionals without one contract.
Frequent mobile and health interactions have high-impact accessibility defects: a 16x16 Habit target, escaping dialog focus, non-modal drawer behavior, and unnamed switches.

quickWins: Add a settings-loading gate before protected health routes.
Tokenize Week View and add two-theme contrast regression coverage.
Expand mobile Habit completion hit areas to at least 44x44 CSS pixels.
Complete the calorie-dialog name, focus trap, inert background, Escape, and focus restoration pattern.
Introduce accessible Switch and IconButton primitives and label health dates.
Disable or implement Export Data and Delete Account.
Move calorie/protein daily status before entry history.
Add previous, Today, and next navigation to daily health logs.
Disable Save Session until workout requirements are met.
Add Retry and an Apply/Edit path to Daily Signals.
Centralize category metadata and make Add respect enabled domains.

structuralChanges.name: Grouped global navigation
structuralChanges.summary: Keep Today and Talk primary; group Plan and Health tools; move utility and advanced destinations out of the flat list.

structuralChanges.name: Today daily decision workspace
structuralChanges.summary: Compose date, load/capacity, focus, obligations, schedule, Anytime, progress, and transparent AI proposals into one hierarchy.

structuralChanges.name: Week master-detail
structuralChanges.summary: Make selected day or All Week drive the agenda and keep recurring Habit cadence aggregated until selected.

structuralChanges.name: Shared Health workspace
structuralChanges.summary: Use one day navigator, units context, status summary, and focused Nutrition, Workouts, and Progress modes.

structuralChanges.name: Mode-based Workouts and category-based Settings
structuralChanges.summary: Replace long equal stacks with Plan/Session/History modes and settings category drill-down.

structuralChanges.name: Frontend module manifest
structuralChanges.summary: Centralize presentation registration for routes, nav, Add, Today, Week, Settings, empty states, permissions, and analytics.

structuralChanges.name: Cross-feature pending actions
structuralChanges.summary: Reuse typed preview, edit, confirm, dismiss, and safe Undo for AI/calendar/health/project planning changes.

structuralChanges.name: Calmer dense visual system
structuralChanges.summary: Preserve cyan and both themes while reducing ambient motion, gradients, glows, pills, and equal card emphasis.

accessibilityIssues: Mobile Habit check-in control measured 16x16 pixels.
Calorie dialog lacks an accessible name and focus containment.
Mobile drawer lacks dialog semantics, focus trap, named controls, and inert background.
Settings toggle buttons lack accessible name, switch role, and checked state.
Calorie and Achievement history icon actions are unnamed.
Health header date inputs are not consistently labelled.
Shared loading spinner has no status/name semantics.
Talk progress and new messages lack live-region status.
Progress bars/donuts lack programmatic progress semantics.
Achievement selection lacks programmatic selected state.
Global text selection is disabled for ordinary content.
designSystemIssues: Week View implements a parallel hard-coded visual system instead of semantic tokens.
No consistent accessible Switch, IconButton, Dialog/Drawer, DayNavigator, Progress, or destructive-recovery primitive.
Cards, borders, rounded corners, glows, gradients, and pills have insufficient hierarchy rules.
Delete confirmation and Undo behavior differs by entity.
Category metadata is duplicated and inconsistent between Add and Edit.
Status colors and module accents are not governed by one semantic model.
Custom focus styling changes with motion preference even though focus visibility is not a motion concern.
Perpetual float, pulse, flicker, and background animation competes with task feedback.
reviewLimitations: The live authenticated walkthrough used the Lina demo persona; other persona days were not reviewed screen by screen.
No connected Google Calendar was available, so OAuth, dense real obligations, sync errors, and disconnect were source-inspected only.
Lina had zero AI credits, so a new live Talk response was not sent; pending actions were inspected in source/tests.
The Lina demo had no duration-target Habit; source was inspected and the existing target-Habit E2E test passed.
Admin Token Manager and Meal OCR Lab were not accessible to the demo user and were code-inspected only.
PWA standalone, install completion, push delivery, offline mode, dictation permission, and full screen-reader sessions were not completed.
Privacy and Terms were mapped but not treated as major workflow screens.
No automated axe suite exists; accessibility evidence came from DOM snapshots, keyboard tests, measurements, code, and visual inspection.
Planned but absent concepts were reported as gaps, not evaluated as implemented workflows.
